steps.name: Create estate DB fixture
steps.notes: wicked-estate index succeeded. Indexed 2 nodes, 1 edge, 1 file. DB created at $TMPDIR/wc-hook-fixture.db, size=172032 bytes. All preconditions (P1-P5) verified: wicked-core and wicked-estate both on PATH, TMPDIR writable, env -i available, no leaked WICKED_* env vars.

steps.name: Case A — gate-hook with valid DB but empty WICKED_DECISIONS_PATH
steps.notes: Command: echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"ls"}}' | WICKED_DECISIONS_PATH="" wicked-core gate-hook --db $TMPDIR/wc-hook-fixture.db. Output line 1: 'wicked-governance: DENY (WICKED_DECISIONS_PATH unset — cannot record decision)'. exit_code=2.

steps.name: Case B — gate-hook with no DB and no env vars
steps.notes: Command: echo '{...}' | env -i PATH="$PATH" wicked-core gate-hook. Output line 1: 'wicked-governance: DENY (no estate store resolvable (set --db or WICKED_ESTATE_DB) — refusing to evaluate against a default/empty store (fail-closed))'. exit_code=2. Contains 'no estate store resolvable', 'fail-closed'. Does not contain 'WICKED_DECISIONS_PATH'.

steps.name: Verify no decisions file was written (negative evidence)
steps.notes: Lock file $TMPDIR/wc-hook-fixture.db.lock does not exist (lockfile_present=1 means ls exited 1). $TMPDIR/wicked-core-gov/ exists and contains 20 pre-existing decisions.ndjson files from prior test runs. Zero decisions.ndjson files were created newer than the test start (step1-fixture.txt timestamp). no_new_decisions_ndjson_during_test=1 confirmed. The gov_dir listing showed prior-run UUIDs only; no new subdirectory was created by the DENY-path invocations in Steps 2 and 3.
